--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

“The internet gave everyone a voice. But it never gave everyone a reputation they could truly own.”
For more than two decades, the internet has transformed how humans communicate, shop, work, and build communities.
Every click, purchase, review, interaction, and contribution creates a digital footprint.
But there is a fundamental problem hiding beneath this progress:
Your digital reputation does not belong to you.
It belongs to platforms.
A five-star rating on one marketplace stays there. Your loyalty status on one application does not follow you elsewhere. Your contributions to a community rarely become a portable asset that proves your credibility across the internet.
The internet remembers your activity.
But it forgets you.
This is the problem is trying to solve by building what it calls a Consumer Reputation Layer a new infrastructure layer designed to transform fragmented consumer activity into a unified, user-owned reputation.
The Internet's Missing Layer: Trust
The internet was originally built around information.
Search engines helped us find information.
Social platforms helped us share information.
Marketplaces helped us exchange value.
But as the internet evolved, one thing became increasingly important:
Trust.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Who is a real user?
Who is a valuable contributor?
Who deserves access?
Who should receive rewards?
Today, most platforms solve this problem independently.
An e-commerce platform has its own rating system.
A social platform has followers and engagement metrics.
A gaming platform has achievements.
A community platform has reputation points.
Each system creates a small island of trust.
But these islands rarely communicate.
“We built a connected internet, but our identities remain disconnected.”
Imagine spending years building a reputation online, only to start from zero every time you enter a new ecosystem.
That is the reality of today's internet.
The Problem With Platform-Owned Reputation
Every day, billions of people create value online.
They:

• Write reviews.

• Recommend products.

• Build communities.

• Participate in campaigns.

• Create content.

• Support brands.

• Contribute knowledge.
But most of that value remains locked inside centralized platforms.
The user creates the reputation.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The platform owns the relationship.This creates several problems

• Reputation Fragmentation
Your digital identity is scattered everywhere.
One platform knows your purchasing behavior.
Another knows your social influence.
Another knows your community contributions.
But there is no unified layer connecting these signals.

• Lack of Ownership
Users spend years building trust, but they cannot carry that trust with them.
Leaving a platform often means leaving behind the reputation you built.

• Increasing Digital Noise
The rise of bots, fake accounts, and artificial engagement makes it harder to distinguish genuine users from manipulation.
In a world where digital interactions continue growing, trust becomes more valuable than ever.
Introducing the Consumer Reputation Layer
A Consumer Reputation Layer is a system that allows user activity and reputation to become:

• Portable.

• Verifiable.

• User-owned.

• Useful across multiple applications.
Instead of every platform asking:
"Who are you?"
A reputation layer allows users to prove:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

"This is what I have done. This is the value I have created. This is the trust I have earned."
Think about it like a digital reputation passport.
Your passport does not belong to every country you visit.
It belongs to you.
Similarly, a consumer reputation layer aims to create an identity foundation that users can carry across digital ecosystems.
CONSO's Vision: Turning Activity Into Reputation
CONSO is building this infrastructure through the idea of a Consumer Passport.
The Consumer Passport is designed to create a unified Consumer Graph by connecting meaningful consumer behaviors across platforms.
Instead of viewing users as isolated accounts, focuses on understanding users through their actions.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

A person's reputation can be shaped by:

• Verified interactions.

• Community participation.

• Consumer behavior.

• Digital contributions.

• Authentic engagement.
The goal is not simply creating another profile.
The goal is creating a reputation layer where activity becomes meaningful.
“The future of identity is not just knowing who someone is. It is understanding the trust they have earned.”
From Reputation to Real Value
A reputation system only becomes powerful when it creates real utility.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

CONSO introduces mechanisms designed to turn reputation into value through concepts like:
♠ ZAPs
ZAPs represent ways users can receive recognition and rewards based on their verified activities and contributions.
♠ Badges
Badges provide reputation signals that represent achievements, participation, or credibility.
♠ Campaigns
Smart Campaigns allow organizations to reach real users based on verified behavior rather than traditional targeting methods.
Instead of asking:
"Who fits this demographic?"
The question becomes:
"Who has demonstrated genuine interest and meaningful participation?"
♠ Payments
A reputation layer could unlock new possibilities where trust becomes part of financial and consumer experiences.
Why Reputation Matters for the Future of AI
Artificial intelligence is creating another major identity challenge.
AI agents are becoming more capable, but intelligence alone is not enough.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The future may require AI systems that understand:

• Context.

• History.

• Preferences.

• Trust.

• Relationships.
A world with millions of AI agents interacting with humans and businesses will need stronger reputation systems.
Without persistent identity and memory, every interaction starts from zero.
The internet may not only need smarter AI.
It may need a trust layer that helps AI understand the humans behind the data.
The Shift From Attention Economy to Trust Economy
For years, the internet rewarded attention.
Clicks.
Views.
Followers.
Virality.
But attention does not always represent value.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

A bot can generate impressions.
A fake account can create engagement.
A purchased following can look influential.
The next evolution of the internet may focus less on:
"How much attention can you attract?"
and more on:
"How much trust have you earned?"
This is where reputation becomes powerful.
A reputation layer creates a foundation where authentic behavior becomes measurable.
The Challenges Ahead
Building a global reputation layer is not simple.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Important questions remain:
♠ Privacy
How can users prove reputation without exposing unnecessary personal information?
♠ Interoperability
How can different platforms contribute and use reputation signals?
♠ Adoption
Will platforms embrace a system where users own more of their digital identity?
These challenges are significant.
But solving difficult infrastructure problems has always been part of technological evolution.
A New Internet Built on Reputation
The first era of the internet was about information.
The second era was about platforms.
The next era may be about ownership and trust.
Users have spent years creating digital value.
They have built communities, supported brands, created content, and contributed knowledge.
The missing piece has been a system that recognizes and connects these contributions.
CONSO’s Consumer Reputation Layer represents a vision where reputation becomes portable, meaningful, and user-owned.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

“The future internet will not only ask what you own. It will ask what you have earned.”
The biggest transformation may not come from creating more platforms.
It may come from creating the infrastructure that allows people to carry their trust everywhere.
And that could change how humans interact online forever.
